career_angle: If you're building AI products, these micro-data marketplaces (Kled AI, Silencio, Neon Mobile) are where training data is heading -- learn their APIs and data formats now. For ML engineers, understanding provenance and consent chains in crowdsourced training data is becoming a compliance-critical skill.

factors.rationale: The Guardian is a Tier 1 publication with editorial standards; Ben's Bites is a respected AI aggregator
Named sources (Jacobus Louw, Sahil Tigga, Ramelio Hill) with specific ages, locations, dollar amounts, and platform names -- all independently verifiable
Kled AI, Silencio, and Neon Mobile are live platforms anyone can download and verify payment claims against
Guardian has no financial stake but the 'at what cost?' framing serves their editorial bias toward tech-skepticism narratives that drive engagement
Data labeling gig economy is well-documented by MIT Tech Review, Rest of World, and academic labor researchers -- nothing novel claimed here
$14 per video, $50 in two weeks, $100/month, $0.50/minute -- precise verifiable figures with named platforms and tasks
Guardian tech desk has strong investigative history; no pattern of fabrication or sensationalism beyond headline choices
